title: rubus sanctus var. vulgaris
intro: Rubus sanctus var. vulgaris, also known as the common blackberry or thorny blackberry, is a variety of the Rubus sanctus species, a type of bramble plant native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Rubus sanctus var. vulgaris include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and treat wounds, as well as its potential to help manage diabetes and certain types of cancer. Rubus sanctus var. vulgaris can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal and health benefits.
